tasks.prompt:
# MISSION
Task 3 proof: repo is clean, dispatch paused, git preflight is green. Verify that after a clean-git start the boundary advances past git_sync to linear_issue readiness. Do not create a Linear issue. Do not launch Jules.

# REQUIRED READING
- `AGENTS.md`
- `.agent/workflows/USER.local.md` if it exists
- `docs/@JULES-WORKFLOW-GUIDE.md`
- `public/agent-docs/rules/Jules.md`
- `public/agent-docs/rules/UPLINK.md` for the Scout/Core bridge and merge protocol
- Relevant source/docs for the files you intend to touch

# EXECUTION STEPS
1. Confirm the repository checkout is based on the current GitHub base branch.
2. Treat `origin/master` at commit `65a5eb93105d7532014934b7da1cae5e63a8f588` as the source of truth for the starting point.
3. Inspect existing code, docs, and local conventions before editing.
4. Keep the implementation bounded to this task and avoid unrelated cleanup.
5. Run the smallest meaningful verification commands and report exactly what passed or failed.
6. Prepare a GitHub PR or equivalent Jules result for Symphony/Scout/Core review.
7. Leave the PR ready for Scout to bridge with arbitration comments before Core validates or merges.

# REQUESTED VERIFICATION
- `npm.cmd run verify:jules-contract`

# EXPECTED WRITE SCOPE
- `conductor/symphony/.symphony/live-proof/git-preflight-clean-2026-05-19.json`
- `conductor/symphony/.symphony/live-proof/dashboard-intake-clean-git-2026-05-19.json`
- `conductor/symphony/.symphony/live-proof/linear-preview-ready-2026-05-19.json`
- `conductor/symphony/.symphony/live-proof/handoff-readiness-linear-boundary-2026-05-19.json`

# CONSTRAINTS
- Do not modify lockfiles such as `package-lock.json` unless dependency changes are explicitly required.
- Avoid central registry/index churn unless this task specifically requires it.
- Preserve unfinished intent and future optionality in Aralia; do not prune systems for neatness.
- If conflict-prone files are unavoidable, call them out in the final report.
- Do not claim visual fixes without rendered verification.

# DELIVERABLE
- Summary of changes made.
- Files changed, with any conflict-prone files called out.
- Verification commands and results.
- PR URL or clear handoff status for Symphony to monitor.
verification: Run `npm.cmd run verify:jules-contract` and report the result.
Run the smallest relevant verification for the files changed.
Report if this task only produced analysis or if no code changes were needed.
Leave enough PR context for Scout to bridge conflicts before Core validates the merge.

Title: Live proof: dashboard-only Jules intake can save a bounded draft

Task details:
Proof-only draft created through the Codex in-app browser. Do not launch Jules from this draft while the GitHub sync gate is blocked. Intended scope is documentation/audit evidence only.

Expected files / write scopes:
- `conductor/symphony/JULES_MIDDLEMAN_AUDIT.md`
- `conductor/symphony/.symphony/live-proof/*`

Requested verification:
- `npm.cmd run verify:jules-contract`

GitHub sync receipt:
- Synced base: `origin/master @ 65a5eb93105d7532014934b7da1cae5e63a8f588`

Acceptance contract for Jules, Scout, and Core:
- Treat Expected files / write scopes as the declared Jules write scope.
- If a PR changes files outside that scope, call that out before merge or local sync.
- Run and report the requested verification commands when code changes.
- Scout and Core should use this issue as the review contract before merge or local sync.

Dashboard control surface:
- Dashboard state: `http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/v1/state`
- Task queue: `http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/v1/task-drafts`

- Assigned issue activity: `http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/v1/<issue>/activity`

Symphony foreman expectations:
- Treat this as a Jules coordination task before treating it as local implementation.
- Verify the Jules starting point is origin/master at commit 65a5eb93105d7532014934b7da1cae5e63a8f588.
- If GitHub sync is blocked, report that blocker instead of starting broad local work.
- Track Jules session, PR checks, conflict-prone files, merge readiness, and local sync back to master.
- Post status comments only on this issue unless the operator asks for broader Linear changes.

tasks.title: Live proof: dashboard-only Jules intake can save a bounded draft
tasks.prompt:
# MISSION
Proof-only draft created through the Codex in-app browser. Do not launch Jules from this draft while the GitHub sync gate is blocked. Intended scope is documentation/audit evidence only.

# REQUIRED READING
- `AGENTS.md`
- `.agent/workflows/USER.local.md` if it exists
- `docs/@JULES-WORKFLOW-GUIDE.md`
- `public/agent-docs/rules/Jules.md`
- `public/agent-docs/rules/UPLINK.md` for the Scout/Core bridge and merge protocol
- Relevant source/docs for the files you intend to touch

# EXECUTION STEPS
1. Confirm the repository checkout is based on the current GitHub base branch.
2. Treat `origin/master` at commit `65a5eb93105d7532014934b7da1cae5e63a8f588` as the source of truth for the starting point.
3. Inspect existing code, docs, and local conventions before editing.
4. Keep the implementation bounded to this task and avoid unrelated cleanup.
5. Run the smallest meaningful verification commands and report exactly what passed or failed.
6. Prepare a GitHub PR or equivalent Jules result for Symphony/Scout/Core review.
7. Leave the PR ready for Scout to bridge with arbitration comments before Core validates or merges.

# REQUESTED VERIFICATION
- `npm.cmd run verify:jules-contract`

# EXPECTED WRITE SCOPE
- `conductor/symphony/JULES_MIDDLEMAN_AUDIT.md`
- `conductor/symphony/.symphony/live-proof/*`

# CONSTRAINTS
- Do not modify lockfiles such as `package-lock.json` unless dependency changes are explicitly required.
- Avoid central registry/index churn unless this task specifically requires it.
- Preserve unfinished intent and future optionality in Aralia; do not prune systems for neatness.
- If conflict-prone files are unavoidable, call them out in the final report.
- Do not claim visual fixes without rendered verification.

# DELIVERABLE
- Summary of changes made.
- Files changed, with any conflict-prone files called out.
- Verification commands and results.
- PR URL or clear handoff status for Symphony to monitor.
verification: Run `npm.cmd run verify:jules-contract` and report the result.
Run the smallest relevant verification for the files changed.
Report if this task only produced analysis or if no code changes were needed.
Leave enough PR context for Scout to bridge conflicts before Core validates the merge.

handoffs.prompt:
# OBSERVED PR
This record watches an existing GitHub pull request for Symphony learning and foreman tracking.
It was not created, staged, or launched by the current dashboard run.

PR: https://github.com/Gambitnl/Aralia/pull/900

blockers: 6 tracked file(s) have uncommitted changes.
17 untracked file(s) are present.
master has 1 unpushed commit(s).
master is behind origin/master by 7 commit(s).
gitPreflight.summary: Blocked: 6 tracked file(s) have uncommitted changes.

resolutionPacket.summary: 1 local-only commit, 7 remote-only commits, 6 tracked changes, 112 untracked files, need disposition before Jules can start from a trustworthy GitHub base.

localCommits.message: feat(encounter): offline bestiary encounter generator with full 5eTools library

remoteCommits.message: Allow Codex Dependabot remediation lockfile PRs

details: Read-only packet. Symphony did not pull, push, stash, clean, reset, or delete anything while generating it.

remediation: Review local changes with `git -C F:\Repos\Aralia status --short`.
Commit and push work that Jules must see; stash, move, or ignore work that should stay local.
Inspect branch divergence with `git -C F:\Repos\Aralia log --oneline --left-right master...origin/master` before choosing a merge, rebase, reset, or push strategy.
Do not start Jules until the local base and GitHub base point at the same commit again.
Re-run Check GitHub Sync after resolving the divergence.

externalReviewComments.author: google-labs-jules
externalReviewComments.body:
👋 Jules, reporting for duty! I'm here to lend a hand with this pull request.

When you start a review, I'll add a 👀 emoji to each comment to let you know I've read it. I'll focus on feedback directed at me and will do my best to stay out of conversations between you and other bots or reviewers to keep the noise down.

I'll push a commit with your requested changes shortly after. Please note there might be a delay between these steps, but rest assured I'm on the job!

For more direct control, you can switch me to **Reactive Mode**. When this mode is on, I will only act on comments where you specifically mention me with `@jules`. You can find this option in the **Pull Request** section of your [global Jules UI settings](https://jules.google.com/settings). You can always switch back!

---
*_For security, I will only act on instructions from the user who triggered this task._*

New to Jules? Learn more at [jules.google/docs](https://jules.google/docs).
